news.summary: ADTRAN Holdings, Inc. unveiled its initial unaudited financial results for Q1 2025. The revenue stands at $247.7 million, reflecting a 10% increase from the previous year and exceeding the mid-point estimation. GAAP gross margin reached 38.5%, while non-GAAP gross margin hit 42.6%. The operating margin aligned with the upper limit of projections. The company reported a GAAP diluted loss per share of $0.13, while non-GAAP diluted earnings pe...
news.headline: ADTRAN Holdings, Inc. reports Q1 2025 revenue of $247.7 million, up 10% YoY. GAAP gross margin: 38.5%, non-GAAP: 42.6%.

news.summary: Bitcoin has surged back above $98,000, hitting this mark for the first time in nearly three months after the US Federal Reserve opted to keep interest rates steady. Despite pressure from President Donald Trump, who criticized the Fed for not cutting rates, Fed chair Jerome Powell highlighted rising risks of higher unemployment and inflation, citing a decline in sentiment due to trade policy concerns. The market expects the Fed funds rate to drop to 3.6% by the end of 2025. Bitcoin initially fell below $96,000 post-Powell's speech but quickly rebounded, reflecting growing momentum. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index returned to "Greed" territory, with Bitcoin ETFs seeing inflows of $4.41 billion since March 26. Economist Timothy Peterson warned that delayed rate cuts in 2025 could lead to broader market downturns, potentially impacting Bitcoin's price. Powell's cautious stance may be based on waiting for more clarity in the future. This article does not offer investment advice; readers are advised to conduct their own research.
news.headline: Bitcoin hits $98K as Fed maintains rates despite Trump's pressure. Powell cites risks of higher unemployment, inflation. BTC rebounds from $95K.

news.summary: The latest statistics indicate that the number of COVID-19 cases has surpassed 10 million globally, with over 500,000 reported deaths. The United States remains the most affected country with over 2.5 million confirmed cases and a death toll exceeding 125,000. Brazil follows closely behind, reporting over 1.3 million cases and more than 57,000 fatalities. Despite efforts to curb the spread of the virus, many countries are still experiencing a rise in cases, emphasizing the importance of following safety guidelines and measures.In addition, countries like India and Russia have also seen a significant increase in cases, with over 570,000 cases in India and around 650,000 cases in Russia. The World Health Organization continues to stress the need for vigilance and cooperation in order to combat the pandemic effectively. It is crucial for individuals to adhere to social distancing protocols, wear masks, and practice good hygiene to prevent further spread of the virus. The global situation remains precarious, and collective action is required to mitigate the impact of COVID-19.
news.headline: Griffin urges US to grant visas to foreign students with degrees - reported by Bloomberg, a leading financial news outlet.

news.summary: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining firm Core Scientific Inc. reported a net profit of $580 million for Q1 2025, a substantial increase from $210 million in the previous year. Despite missing revenue estimates by 8.11% with total revenues of $79.5 million (down from $179.3 million in Q1 2024), the company attributes the drop to reduced mining profits after the Bitcoin halving on April 20, 2024. Core Scientific's revenue composition included $67.2 million from self-mining, $3.8 million in hosted mining, and $8.6 million in colocation revenue. The shift towards hosting high-performance computing (HPC) for AI purposes has partially offset losses, with operational efficiencies compensating for reduced mining rewards. This strategic pivot is exemplified by a $1.2 billion deal with AI startup CoreWeave, aiming for annualized colocation revenue of $360 million in 2026. Core Scientific CEO Adam Sullivan views this period as an "inflection point" aligning with global demand for high-performance data infrastructure. VanEck estimates a potential $13.9 billion increase in yearly profits by 2027 if miners transition 20% of energy capacity to AI and HPC. Other mining firms like Riot Platforms, Hive Digital, Hut 8, Iris Energy, and TeraWulf have similarly ventured into HPC and AI, showcasing industry-wide movement towards this sector. The article emphasizes Core Scientific's ability to navigate market trends and capitalize on emerging opportunities within the crypto mining and data infrastructure landscape.
news.headline: Core Scientific's Q1 profits hit $580M but miss revenue forecast. Net income doubled to $210M, revenue $79.5M, missing estimates by 8.11%. Bitcoin rewards halving led to the drop in profits, partially offset by higher BTC price and reduced power costs. Shift to HPC hosting causes losses. Shares of Core Scientific down 1% in May 7 trading, but rose 3% post-market. Company to end 2026 with $360M in annual colocation revenue. Bitcoin miners transitioning to AI and HPC could earn $13.9B more yearly by 2027. Other mining firms engaging in similar shifts. Adam Sullivan sees Q1 as turning point for Core Scientific in AI transition.

news.summary: According to the latest report, the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.2% in the month of June, marking a significant decrease from the previous month's rate of 5.0%. The number of jobs added in June was reported to be 250,000, exceeding economists' expectations. This decrease in unemployment and rise in job creation has been attributed to the reopening of businesses and the overall economic recovery following the pandemic. The labor force participation rate also saw an increase to 61.5%, indicating more people are actively seeking employment. The positive trend in the labor market is a promising sign for the country's economy as it continues to bounce back from the challenges of the past year.
news.headline: $450M worth of Green Bonds announced by Allied.

news.summary: Sweden, as a member of the EU, makes monthly financial contributions to the EU's budget. The Swedish government pays these contributions in Swedish kronor, which are then exchanged for euros in the foreign exchange market by a central bank in the EU.
news.headline: Sweden makes monthly payments to EU in SEK, exchanged into euro by recipient central bank within EU.

news.summary: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) data released on August 14, 2024, indicates a more stable inflation rate, which may lead to the Federal Reserve commencing the process of cutting interest rates in September. This development suggests a favorable economic environment, as hinted by economist Lawrence Yun.
news.headline: CPI report on August 14, 2024 shows calmer inflation, paving way for Fed rate cuts in September, says Lawrence Yun.

news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that the economy expanded by 3.5% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations. This growth was driven by a surge in consumer spending, which increased by 6.2%. Additionally, business investments rose by 4.8%, marking a significant boost in economic activity. The unemployment rate also dropped to 4.1%, the lowest it has been in the past decade. These numbers indicate a strong and growing economy, with positive signs for future prosperity.
news.headline: Allied finalizes $450M green bond issuance.

news.summary: The latest report on mortgage rates as of August 15, 2024, shows that the 30-year fixed mortgage rate has seen minimal change. news.summary: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. is offering up to $25.0 million in Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds (Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Project) Series 2022A-2. This offering represents the drawdown of the remainder of the initial $35.0 million loaned to the Company in June 2022. The Bonds will be guaranteed by Casella's subsidiaries and the net proceeds will be used to fund capital projects in the State of Vermont. The offering is part of a larger financing strategy for Casella's waste, recycling, and resource management services.
news.headline: Casella offers $25.0 million in Vermont bonds for waste disposal projects, aims to fund state capital projects.

news.summary: On March 6, 2025, Casella Waste Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CWST), a company providing waste management services, announced the pricing of an offering of $25.0 million aggregate principal amount of Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds (Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Project) Series 2022A-2. These bonds are part of an indenture dated June 1, 2022, between the Issuer and the bond trustee. The Bonds signify the drawdown of the remaining funds from the Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds (Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Project) Series 2022, initially providing $35.0 million to the Company in June 2022. Failure to utilize the drawdown by June 1, 2025 would result in the funds no longer being available to the company.
news.headline: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. priced $25.0 million bonds for waste disposal project in Vermont, total loaned amount $60.0 million.

news.summary: Alaska Air Group, Inc. (NYSE: ALK) saw its shares decline in premarket trading on Thursday. The company's first-quarter sales grew by 41% year over year, reaching $3.14 billion, falling short of the expected $3.17 billion. The Seattle-based company revealed a 3.9% year-over-year increase in capacity (available seat miles), surpassing expectations by approximately 1 point due to lower flight cancellation rates. Operating revenue per available seat mile (RASM) rose by 5.0% compared to the previous year. Unit revenues also climbed by 5.0%, despite a 3-point negative impact from weakening demand. Unit costs increased by 2.1% year over year, in line with the company's forecasts, including expenses related to the newly ratified Alaska flight attendant contract in February. Economic fuel price per gallon stood at $2.61 in the first quarter, reflecting the influence of moderating crude oil prices.
news.headline: Alaska Air Group reports 41% Y/Y sales growth to $3.14 billion in Q1, missing estimates. Unit revenues up 5.0%, unit costs rise 2.1%. Unit revenues improved despite 3-point softening demand impact. Full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: In Des Moines, Iowa, Curql, a group of credit unions investing in fintech together, has announced that their Curql Fund II has raised over $300 million from 83 innovative credit unions nationwide. The fund is expected to reach its closing at the end of July, marking credit unions' last chance to join in this initiative. This movement, driven by credit unions and for credit unions, aims to transform the financial services industry. Curql Fund I had 64 credit unions participating, demonstrating the growing impact and interest in this collective effort.
